rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GARY Y. TAKEOKA
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: CONTINUED TO 12/29/2025. NO HEARING ON 12/22/2025 BECAUSE OF THE POWER OUTAGE AT CIVIC CENTER COURTHOUSE. DEPT. 204 WILL REMAIN CLOSED UNTIL FURTHER NOTICE. PLEASE CHECK BACK LATER FOR A NEW HEARING DATE. Hearing. Appearance required for the judicial officer to review and consider whether to take this petition off calendar, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 08/27/2025): 1) Re the DE-120 form (notice of hearing) filed on 06/23/2025: i) Item 1 must specify petitioner's capacity as "executor". ii) Direct notice must be given to each minor beneficiary of the devisee trust. See CRC 7.51(d). iii) Separate notice must be given to each person having legal custody of and residing with each minor of the devisee trust. See CRC 7.51(d). A verified allegation must be made to identify each person having legal custody of and residing with each minor. iv) Solomon Fong and Nolan Yamada appear to be adults now. Direct notice must be given to each. v) Lowell High School's address is different from its address listed on the case-initiating petition for probate. A verified allegation must be made to confirm the current address. 2) An allegation must be made as to whether there are any assignments or powers of attorney that may affect distribution. See LRSF 14.35(J)(1). 3) Has petitioner taken any IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250(a)? 4) Please provide proof of compliance of Probate Code section 10533(a)(1) or (2) showing that the estate cash on hand is fully insured or is otherwise held in the form of authorized investment(s). 5) The decedent died more than 2 years ago, but no first interim account has been filed. See LRSF 14.34(A) limiting the first account period to 2 years from the date of death; see also section 10950(a) re the Court's authority to order the first account on its own motion. If petitioner in her capacity as trustee of the devisee trust has waived the first account, please file a signed waiver of account. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARION HALL REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/28/2026. Issues necessitating a continuance: 1) The ending balance of cash assets in accounts must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. The statements or letters must encompass, not precede, the ending date of the accounting period (LRSF 14.34.D). 2) The summary of account indicates disbursements of $4,117,420.83, but the total disbursements in schedule 2 total just $117,420.83. Please address the above issues and provide courtesy copies of all future filings to this examiner no later than 01/14/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/6/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Courtesy copies and a revised proposed order were not received by the assigned examiner by the 12/8/25 deadline. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Lack of courtesy copies delays review. (2) An amended petition was filed on 12/8/25 instead, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. (3) Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (4) It does not appear that the petition or the amended petition included the disclosures required by Probate Code Sections 1064(a)(4) and (5). DEADLINE to provide the assigned examiner courtesy copies of the amended petition and any papers filed to address these issues is 3/23/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although no proof has been filed that post-deceased nephew Stephen M. Cotter's will has been admitted to probate. Letters will have to have been issued to the executor of his will or the administrator with will annexed for his estate before any distribution is ordered in this estate. Status of closing the estate will be set for 6/21/27. The administrator must file either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before 6/21/27. Appearance will be required on that status date if no petition has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE BRUCE M. COWAN TESTAMENTARY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order To 1)Appoint Trustee Of Testamentary Trust; 2) For Recovery Of Testamentary Trust Funds, And 3) For Instructions To Make Outright D
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/02/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) Form DE-115 is the mandatory form for this type of petition (Probate Code Section 851). 2) All notices must include language about remote appearances (San Francisco Local Rule 14.1.C.6). 3) A petition involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries (CRC 7.902). 4) For the court to appoint a trustee, the petitioner must name a fiduciary who agrees to act. 5) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A). Please address the above issues and deliver courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 01/20/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BRIAN VANCE EVANS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/20/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) If the whereabouts of interested parties are unknown, a declaration must be filed describing efforts made to locate the persons entitled to notice. If, after diligent search, such persons cannot be located, an ex parte petition must be filed to request that notice to such persons be dispensed with. (2) If the father's death has not been confirmed, the petitioner might need to file a petition to determine fact of death. Deadline to deliver courtesy copies to the assigned examiner of any papers filed to address these issues is 4/6/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
